SEARCH
Package neqsim.process.optimization.valuechain

Class ValueChainObjective

java.lang.Object
neqsim.process.optimization.valuechain.ValueChainObjective
All Implemented Interfaces:
Serializable
public class ValueChainObjective extends Object implements Serializable
Converts a converged process operating point into a single economic objective value.

This is the foundational value-chain objective: it rolls produced sales streams into revenue, consumed energy into operating cost, and the carbon associated with that energy into a carbon cost, then returns the net daily value (and, for life-of-field work, the present value of an annual cash flow). It is an open analogue of the "activated economics" layer found in commercial flowsheet optimizers, but it lives inside the optimization loop so it can be used directly as the objective of AgenticProcessOptimizer or any of the value-chain optimizers in this package.

The class is deliberately decoupled from the flowsheet: callers pass already-computed physical quantities (export gas rate, oil rate, total shaft/electrical power) so the objective is pure, fast, and trivially testable. The carbon price can be overridden per call to sweep a CO 2-price axis and build a revenue-versus-emissions Pareto front without re-running the simulation. 

 ValueChainObjective obj = new ValueChainObjective(econ);
 ValueChainObjective.ValueResult r = obj.evaluate(3.2e6, 0.0, 4500.0);
 double npv = obj.presentValueOfAnnualCashFlow(r.getNetValueNokPerDay(), 0);
Version:
1.0
Author:
NeqSim Development Team

  • Constructor Details

    • ValueChainObjective

      public ValueChainObjective(EconomicParameters econ)
      Creates a value-chain objective backed by the supplied economic parameters.
      Parameters:
      econ - the economic parameters (must not be null)

  • Method Details

    • getEconomicParameters

      public EconomicParameters getEconomicParameters()
      Gets the economic parameters backing this objective.
      Returns:
      the economic parameters

    • evaluate

      public ValueChainObjective.ValueResult evaluate(double exportGasSm3PerDay, double exportOilSm3PerDay, double totalPowerKw)
      Evaluates the net daily economic value of an operating point using the configured carbon price.
      Parameters:
      exportGasSm3PerDay - export sales-gas rate in Sm3/day (non-negative)
      exportOilSm3PerDay - export oil/condensate rate in Sm3/day (non-negative; 0 for dry gas)
      totalPowerKw - total consumed shaft/electrical power in kW (non-negative)
      Returns:
      the value breakdown for this operating point

    • evaluate

      public ValueChainObjective.ValueResult evaluate(double exportGasSm3PerDay, double exportOilSm3PerDay, double totalPowerKw, double carbonPriceNokPerTonne)
      Evaluates the net daily economic value of an operating point with an explicit carbon price.

      Energy is converted to CO2 via the configured carbon intensity (tonnes/MWh). Sweep carbonPriceNokPerTonne to trace a revenue-versus-emissions Pareto front for a fixed operating point.

      Parameters:
      exportGasSm3PerDay - export sales-gas rate in Sm3/day (non-negative)
      exportOilSm3PerDay - export oil/condensate rate in Sm3/day (non-negative)
      totalPowerKw - total consumed shaft/electrical power in kW (non-negative)
      carbonPriceNokPerTonne - carbon price in currency per tonne CO2 (non-negative)
      Returns:
      the value breakdown for this operating point

    • presentValueOfAnnualCashFlow

      public double presentValueOfAnnualCashFlow(double netValueNokPerDay, double year)
      Computes the present value of an annual cash flow received in a given year.

      The daily net value is annualised over PRODUCTION_DAYS_PER_YEAR and discounted to the present using the configured discount rate.

      Parameters:
      netValueNokPerDay - the net daily value in currency per day
      year - the year in which the cash flow is received (0 = present)
      Returns:
      the present value of one year of cash flow in the configured currency
